contentBlocks.original:
ॐ एकदंताय विद्महे वक्रतुण्डाय धीमहि
तन्नो दंतिः प्रचोदयात्
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ekadantāya vidmahe vakratuṇḍāya dhīmahi
tanno dantiḥ pracodayāt
contentBlocks.translation: We pray to the one-tusked one; we meditate on the one having a twisted trunk. May that Ganapati (one-tusked) inspire us (to meditate).
contentBlocks.meanings: ॐ (oṃ) – sacred syllable; एकदंताय (ekadantāya) – to the one-tusked (eka:one | danta:tusk | āya:to); विद्महे (vidmahe) – we meditate; वक्रतुण्डाय (vakratuṇḍāya) – to the twisted trunk (vakra:twisted | tuṇḍa:trunk | āya:to); धीमहि (dhīmahi) – we meditate; तन्नः (tannoḥ) – may that (tat:that | naḥ:us); दंतिः (dantiḥ) – Ganapati (one-tusked); प्रचोदयात् (pracodayāt) – inspire;
